steps.title: Set w(r) equal to zero
steps.content:
When $w(r) = 0$, we have:
$$\frac{1}{r-8} - \frac{r-5}{-r+4.25} = 0$$
Move the second fraction to the right side:
$$\frac{1}{r-8} = \frac{r-5}{-r+4.25}$$

steps.title: Simplify the right side and cross-multiply
Expand and form a quadratic equation
steps.content:
Expand $(r-5)(r-8) = r^2 - 13r + 40$:
$$r - 4.25 = -r^2 + 13r - 40$$
Rearrange to standard form:
$$r^2 - 12r + 35.75 = 0$$
Multiply by 4 to clear the decimal:
$$4r^2 - 48r + 143 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve using the quadratic formula
steps.content:
With $a = 4$, $b = -48$, and $c = 143$:
$$r = \frac{48 \pm \sqrt{2304 - 2288}}{8} = \frac{48 \pm \sqrt{16}}{8} = \frac{48 \pm 4}{8}$$
The two solutions are $r = \frac{52}{8} = \frac{13}{2}$ and $r = \frac{44}{8} = \frac{11}{2}$. The greatest possible value is $\frac{13}{2}$ or $6.5$.

Equivalent forms accepted: 13/2, 6.5.
